set.code:
SELECT name, email
FROM users
WHERE city = 'Roma';
-- Returns matching rows

INSERT INTO users (id, name, email)
VALUES (1, 'Marco', 'marco@email.com');

-- SQL: Structured Query Language
SELECT name, age
FROM users
WHERE age >= 18;

SELECT name, city
FROM users
WHERE age > 18
  AND active = true
  AND city IS NOT NULL;

SELECT name, age
FROM users
WHERE age BETWEEN 18 AND 65
ORDER BY age;

SELECT
  name,
  COALESCE(nickname, name) AS display_name
FROM users;

SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month,
  COUNT(*)
FROM orders
GROUP BY month;

SELECT
  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM created_at) AS year,
  EXTRACT(MONTH FROM created_at) AS month
FROM orders;

SELECT name, country
FROM users
WHERE country IN ('IT', 'FR', 'DE')
ORDER BY name;

INSERT INTO users (name, email, age)
VALUES ('Marco', 'marco@email.com', 30);

SELECT name, email
FROM users
WHERE name LIKE 'M%'
ORDER BY name;

SELECT name, email
FROM users
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 10;

SELECT name, email
FROM users
ORDER BY name
LIMIT 10 OFFSET 20;

-- Page 3 (20 items per page)
SELECT name, email
FROM users
ORDER BY id
LIMIT 20 OFFSET 40;

UPDATE users
SET name = 'Mario',
    age = 31,
    updated_at = NOW()
WHERE id = 5;

UPDATE users
SET active = false,
    updated_at = NOW()
WHERE last_login < '2025-01-01';

INSERT INTO users (email, name)
VALUES ('marco@email.com', 'Marco')
ON CONFLICT (email)
DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name;

INSERT INTO users (id, name)
VALUES
  (1, 'Anna'),
  (2, 'Luca'),
  (3, 'Eva');

SELECT name, email
FROM users
WHERE active = true
  AND age >= 18;

SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total,
  AVG(price) AS avg_price,
  MAX(price) AS max_price
FROM products;

SELECT u.name AS user_name,
       o.total AS order_total
FROM users u
JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id;

SELECT name,
  CASE
    WHEN age >= 18 THEN 'adult'
    ELSE 'minor'
  END AS category
FROM users;

SELECT id FROM users
EXCEPT
SELECT user_id FROM orders;
-- Users who never ordered

SELECT a.name, b.name
FROM employees a
FULL OUTER JOIN departments b
  ON a.dept_id = b.id;

SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS total
FROM users
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY total DESC;

SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS total
FROM users
GROUP BY country
HAVING COUNT(*) > 100;

SELECT u.name, o.total, o.created_at
FROM users u
INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
WHERE o.total > 100;

SELECT u.name, o.total
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
WHERE o.id IS NULL;
-- Users with no orders

SELECT name, created_at
FROM users
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 20;

SELECT e.name AS employee,
       m.name AS manager
FROM employees e
JOIN employees m ON e.manager_id = m.id;

SELECT user_id,
  SUM(amount) AS total_spent
FROM orders
GROUP BY user_id;

SELECT name, email FROM users
UNION
SELECT name, email FROM customers
ORDER BY name;

CREATE TABLE posts (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  title VARCHAR(200),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  created_by INT REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE comments (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  body TEXT,
  commentable_type VARCHAR(50),
  commentable_id INT
);
-- Can reference posts, photos, etc.

CREATE TABLE order_items (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  price DECIMAL(10,2),
  quantity INT,
  total DECIMAL GENERATED ALWAYS AS
    (price * quantity) STORED
);

CREATE TABLE daily_sales AS
SELECT
  DATE(created_at) AS sale_date,
  SUM(amount) AS total
FROM sales
GROUP BY sale_date;

CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_date
  ON orders(user_id, created_at);
-- Optimizes: WHERE user_id = 1
--   AND created_at > '2025-01-01'

-- Build index without locking writes
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY
  idx_users_city
  ON users(city);

CREATE INDEX idx_orders_cover
  ON orders(user_id)
  INCLUDE (total, status);
-- Index-only scan, no table lookup

CREATE INDEX idx_pending_orders
  ON orders(id)
  WHERE status = 'pending';
-- Only indexes pending orders

SELECT indexrelname, idx_scan
FROM pg_stat_user_indexes
WHERE idx_scan = 0
ORDER BY pg_relation_size(indexrelid) DESC;

UPDATE products
SET price = 29.99,
    version = version + 1
WHERE id = 1 AND version = 5;
-- Fails if version changed

SELECT u.name, u.email
FROM users u
WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM orders o
  WHERE o.user_id = u.id
    AND o.total > 500
);

WITH active_users AS (
  SELECT id, name
  FROM users
  WHERE active = true
)
SELECT * FROM active_users
ORDER BY name;

SELECT sub.user_id, sub.order_count
FROM (
  SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count
  FROM orders
  GROUP BY user_id
) sub
WHERE sub.order_count > 5;

SELECT u.name, recent.total
FROM users u,
LATERAL (
  SELECT total FROM orders
  WHERE user_id = u.id
  ORDER BY created_at DESC
  LIMIT 3
) recent;

WITH
  active AS (SELECT * FROM users WHERE active),
  totals AS (
    SELECT user_id, SUM(amount) AS total
    FROM orders GROUP BY user_id
  )
SELECT a.name, t.total
FROM active a JOIN totals t ON a.id = t.user_id;

SELECT u.name
FROM users u
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM orders o
  WHERE o.user_id = u.id
);
-- Users with no orders

SELECT user_id,
  amount,
  SUM(amount) OVER (
    PARTITION BY user_id
    ORDER BY created_at
  ) AS running_total
FROM orders;

WITH RECURSIVE tree AS (
  SELECT id, name, parent_id, 0 AS depth
  FROM categories WHERE parent_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT c.id, c.name, c.parent_id, t.depth + 1
  FROM categories c JOIN tree t ON c.parent_id = t.id
)
SELECT * FROM tree;

SELECT name,
  (SELECT COUNT(*)
   FROM orders
   WHERE user_id = u.id) AS order_count
FROM users u;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON users
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

CREATE VIEW active_users AS
SELECT id, name, email
FROM users
WHERE active = true
  AND deleted_at IS NULL;

SELECT name, salary,
  AVG(salary) OVER (PARTITION BY dept)
    AS dept_avg
FROM employees;

WITH recent_orders AS (
  SELECT * FROM orders
  WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
)
SELECT user_id, SUM(total)
FROM recent_orders
GROUP BY user_id;

SET user:1 '{"name":"Marco"}'
SET user:2 '{"name":"Anna"}'
GET user:1

-- MongoDB collection operations
db.users.find()
db.users.countDocuments()
db.users.drop()

HSET user:1 name "Marco" age 30 city "Roma"
HGET user:1 name
HGETALL user:1

LPUSH queue "task_3"
LPUSH queue "task_2"
LPUSH queue "task_1"
RPOP queue
-- Returns "task_3"

SET user:1 "Marco"
SET user:1:email "marco@email.com"
GET user:1

SADD online_users "marco"
SADD online_users "anna"
SISMEMBER online_users "marco"
-- Returns 1 (true)

XADD events * type click page /home
XADD events * type click page /about
XLEN events
-- Returns 2

ZADD leaderboard 100 "marco"
ZADD leaderboard 95 "anna"
ZADD leaderboard 88 "luca"
ZRANGE leaderboard 0 -1 WITHSCORES

ZADD scores 95 "alice" 88 "bob" 72 "carol"
ZRANK scores "alice"
ZREVRANGE scores 0 2 WITHSCORES

EXPLAIN
SELECT name, email
FROM users
WHERE id = 1;

EXPLAIN ANALYZE
SELECT u.name, COUNT(o.id)
FROM users u
JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
WHERE u.active = true
GROUP BY u.name;

SELECT query, calls, total_exec_time
FROM pg_stat_statements
ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC
LIMIT 10;
